message:
Wire browser ready-handoff limit into scheduler and builder

Implement the runtime plumbing for the browser_ready_handoff_limit
config field, adding the builder API and full three-lane scheduler
integration that enforces burst fairness for browser event-loop
adapters.

src/runtime/builder.rs:
- Add browser_ready_handoff_limit() builder method with doc comment
  explaining the one-shot handoff semantics and browser use case
- Wire the config value into the scheduler during RuntimeInner
  construction via set_browser_ready_handoff_limit()
- Add builder method to the configuration reference table

src/runtime/scheduler/three_lane.rs:
- Add DEFAULT_BROWSER_READY_HANDOFF_LIMIT constant (0, disabled)
- Add browser_ready_handoff_limit field to ThreeLaneScheduler and
  ThreeLaneWorker, initialized from the constant
- Add ready_dispatch_streak counter to ThreeLaneWorker, tracking
  consecutive ready-lane dispatches across all ready paths
  (fast_queue, local_ready, global ready, steal, local scheduler)
- Add browser_ready_handoff_yields counter to PreemptionMetrics
- Implement set_browser_ready_handoff_limit() on the scheduler,
  propagating the limit to all workers and resetting streaks when
  the feature is disabled
- Implement should_force_ready_handoff() on the worker: returns true
  when the streak meets the limit AND more ready work is pending
  (checked across fast_queue, global ready, local_ready, and local
  scheduler queues), preventing false yields when queues are empty
- Insert handoff check between phase 2 (priority lanes) and phase 3
  (fast ready paths) of the dispatch loop, returning None to yield
  the host turn when triggered
- Reset ready_dispatch_streak on all non-ready dispatch paths
  (cancel, timed, backoff/park) and increment it on every ready
  dispatch path (fast_queue, local_ready, global ready, local
  scheduler, steal)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
